task: Build with small Lego blocks for the nth time with n blocks
raw:
SCENE ANALYSIS - A white tray holds a small pile of colorful small Lego blocks. The blocks are loose and accessible. STRATEGY - First, grasp a block from the pile. Then, transport it to a new location on the tray. Finally, place it down. This sequence builds a structure of one block. Failure modes include dropping the block during transport or placing it incorrectly. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1 reach the pile of blocks to grasp one. Step 2 grasp a single block from the pile. Step 3 transport the block to a target spot on the tray. Step 4 place the block at the target spot.
Goal: stacked([block_a])
Scene: tray, small_lego_blocks
Step 1: reach(tray)
  affordance_region: the surface of the white tray
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, tray, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, tray) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(block_a)
  affordance_region: a single small Lego block from the pile on the tray
  contact: holding(block_a) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(block_a)
Step 3: transport(block_a -> tray)
  affordance_region: an empty area on the white tray
  spatial: above(block_a, tray, 0.05) [completion]
  safety: no_drop(block_a)
  done_when: above(block_a, tray, 0.05)
Step 4: place(block_a -> tray)
  affordance_region: an empty area on the white tray
  contact: surface_contact(block_a, tray) [completion]; released(block_a) [completion]
  pose: stable(block_a) [completion]
  done_when: surface_contact(block_a, tray) AND released(block_a)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the surface of the white tray
a single small Lego block from the pile on the tray
an empty area on the white tray
an empty area on the white tray
